turnaround_reason: Stock fell 60%+ from $63 highs after Q4 2025 showed a net loss driven by elevated benefits costs crushing gross margins. Thesis: CEO Sarvadi's cumulative $15.5M insider buying (March + June 2026) signals conviction margin recovery is coming via pricing actions, UnitedHealthcare plan redesigns, and HRScale (Workday-powered) rollout. PEO industry remains structurally growing.
bullish_points: CEO Sarvadi bought $4.7M in March at ~$23 and another $7.9M in June at $34.05 — his largest-ever purchases, after having sold at $52 in Sept 2025. That's not window dressing; that's a dramatic re-entry
Cluster insider buying: CFO, general counsel, and 8+ directors also bought in March 2026
Forward P/E ~13.6x on a business with $6.8B in revenue and structural PEO demand is reasonable if margins normalize
HR360 (full-service PEO) + new HRScale (Workday-powered) product for mid-market provides growth lever beyond cost recovery
Management guided for significant profit recovery in 2026, citing improved pricing, plan design changes with UHC, and client selection discipline
bearish_points: Debt-to-equity 641x (driven by $430M debt on thin equity after net losses) — balance sheet is fragile
Benefits costs may remain elevated longer than expected; healthcare cost trend is structural, not one-time
Q4 2025: net loss $33M, adj EBITDA -$13M, gross profit down 21% — the damage was severe
Only 4 analysts cover the stock; consensus target $39.50 is barely above current price — limited institutional conviction
Gross margin ~13% TTM is razor-thin for a PEO; even a small miss on benefits cost normalization keeps margins compressed
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (Jul 30) — first clean quarter with pricing actions + UHC plan redesigns fully in effect; need to see gross margin step-up toward 15%+
suggested_trade: Oct 2026 $35/$45 call spread — leverages margin recovery catalyst with capped risk. Alternatively, spot position with stop at $30.
key_risk: Benefits costs don't normalize in Q2, gross margins stay compressed, and the CEO's massive buy becomes a sunk-cost anchor rather than a signal
would_skip_if: If Q2 earnings show gross margin still below 14% with no clear trajectory to 15%+, the recovery timeline extends and the setup loses urgency
